# `intersect` for [filters](/commands/categories/filters.md)

<div class='command-title'>Returns a list of unique elements present in both the input and the provided list.</div>

## Signature

```> intersect {flags} (other)```

## Parameters

 -  `other`: The other list to intersect with.


## Input/output types:

| input     | output    |
| --------- | --------- |
| list&lt;any&gt; | list&lt;any&gt; |
| table     | table     |
## Examples

Return the intersection of two lists
```nu
> [1 2 3 4] | intersect [3 4 5 6]
╭───┬───╮
│ 0 │ 3 │
│ 1 │ 4 │
╰───┴───╯

```

Intersection with no common elements
```nu
> [1 2 3] | intersect [4 5 6]
╭────────────╮
│ empty list │
╰────────────╯

```

Intersection of two tables
```nu
> [{a:1} {a:2} {a:3}] | intersect [{a:2} {a:3} {a:4}]
╭───┬───╮
│ # │ a │
├───┼───┤
│ 0 │ 2 │
│ 1 │ 3 │
╰───┴───╯

```
